methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Runway is a Series C–stage AI & Software company with a last post-money valuation of $1.5B and total raised exceeding $240M. The peer universe comprises 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Figure AI, Skild AI, Luma AI, Magic AI) alongside 1 public reference point (Palantir), providing a mixed but relevant comp set for triangulation.

Two methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C to subsequent rounds, implies $2.5B. Peer-Median across the 4 same-stage AI & Software peers yields $9.0B, though the underlying range is wide ($1.5B to $39.0B), reflecting significant valuation dispersion within the cohort. The peer-median approach is materially sensitive to composition, with Figure AI at $39.0B pulling the median upward.

Triangulation across both methods yields a range of $2.2B (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.8B. The midpoint is nearly 4x the current $1.5B valuation, reflecting market expectations for frontier-tech momentum. Key uncertainties center on peer-set stability—the 26x spread between min and max peer valuations signals that stage and sector alone are weak predictors—and whether Runway's specific revenue trajectory, user engagement, or technical differentiation justifies positioning toward the median or the tighter stage-progression result. Further diligence on run rate and unit economics is warranted to narrow this band.
